description: A drug smuggling ring based in Indianapolis, Indiana, operating in the late 1970s and early 1980s. Organized by Linda Leary and her sons Richard and Paul Heilbrunn, it smuggled an estimated 250,000 pounds of marijuana from Colombia, Jamaica, and Thailand into eleven midwestern states, valued at $50-100 million. The ring used a health-food business as a front and laundered money through offshore accounts.

country_links.evidence_quote: In the second half of 1985 Linda and her sons fled to Austria after learning a federal grand jury was looking into allegations the Heilbrunns were directing a multi-state smuggling operation.
country_links.context: The organization's leaders fled to Austria to avoid prosecution.

country_links.evidence_quote: Millions of dollars were laundered in offshore accounts and fake companies in the Bahamas and Panama .
country_links.context: The organization laundered money through offshore accounts and fake companies in the Bahamas.

country_links.evidence_quote: Jenkins escaped and was eventually captured in a small Canadian town; he hanged himself in a British Columbia jail.
country_links.context: A member of the organization (Jenkins) was captured in a small Canadian town and later died in a British Columbia jail.

country_links.evidence_quote: Prosecutors estimated that the gang ran an estimated 250,000 pounds of Colombian , Jamaican and Thai marijuana .
country_links.context: The organization sourced marijuana from Colombia.
